title: Announcing the Agent2Agent Protocol (A2A)
quote: Google announced A2A on 2025-04-09 as an open protocol for agent interoperability, built on HTTP, SSE, and JSON-RPC with long-running task support.

title: Google Cloud donates A2A to Linux Foundation
quote: On 2025-06-23 Google said A2A was transferred to a Linux Foundation-hosted project with neutral governance and community stewardship.

title: Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol Specification
quote: The latest released A2A specification is 1.0.0 and defines discovery, modality negotiation, task management, and secure information exchange between independent agents.

title: Agent Discovery
quote: A2A discovery supports well-known Agent Cards at /.well-known/agent-card.json, curated registries, and direct configuration patterns.

title: Enterprise Implementation of A2A
quote: Production A2A should run over HTTPS, advertise auth in the Agent Card, use HTTP-layer credentials, and integrate with tracing, auditing, and API management.

title: A2A and MCP
quote: A2A and MCP are complementary: A2A is for stateful inter-agent collaboration, while MCP is more about agents using capabilities.

title: Extensions in A2A
quote: Extensions can add data, requirements, RPC methods, and state machines, but they should not fragment or replace the core protocol.

title: Agent2Agent (A2A) Project
quote: The official project lists the core A2A spec plus SDKs for Python, JS/TS, Java, Go, .NET, and Rust, along with samples, inspector, and TCK.

title: A2A Python SDK
quote: The official Python SDK supports FastAPI or Starlette, gRPC, OpenTelemetry, and SQL backends; its public README still highlights spec 0.3 with a separate 1.0-dev track.

title: A2A JavaScript SDK
quote: The official JavaScript SDK shows an Express-based server using AgentExecutor, DefaultRequestHandler, InMemoryTaskStore, and JSON-RPC, REST, and gRPC transports.

title: Agent Skills & Agent Card
quote: An Agent Skill declares id, name, description, tags, examples, media types, and security requirements; the Agent Card exposes those capabilities for discovery.

title: The Agent Executor
quote: The AgentExecutor is the core request-processing abstraction and emits Task, Message, TaskStatusUpdateEvent, and TaskArtifactUpdateEvent objects through an event queue.

title: a2a.server.request_handlers.default_request_handler module
quote: DefaultRequestHandler coordinates the AgentExecutor, TaskStore, QueueManager, and optional push notifier across the standard A2A methods.

title: a2a.server.request_handlers.rest_handler module
quote: The REST handler maps REST-like JSON+HTTP requests to the request handler methods and keeps them equivalent to the gRPC service definitions.

title: A2A Protocol Surpasses 150 Organizations, Lands in Major Cloud Platforms, and Sees Enterprise Production Use in First Year
quote: The Linux Foundation said on 2026-04-09 that A2A had more than 150 supporting organizations, major cloud integrations, and active production deployments across multiple industries.

title: Deploy A2A servers in AgentCore Runtime
quote: Amazon Bedrock AgentCore says its A2A support acts as a transparent proxy layer, preserves Agent Cards and JSON-RPC, and adds enterprise authentication and scalability.

title: Add an A2A agent endpoint to Foundry Agent Service
quote: Microsoft documents an A2A tool for Foundry Agent Service and notes that some A2A-related SDK surface is currently in preview.

title: Agent2Agent (A2A) authentication
quote: Microsoft says most A2A endpoints require authentication and describes shared authentication, individual authentication, key-based auth, managed identity, and OAuth passthrough patterns.

title: A2A Protocol Technology Compatibility Kit (TCK)
quote: The A2A TCK separates mandatory, capability, transport-equivalence, quality, and feature checks, and states that quality tests assess production readiness but do not block compliance by default.

title: A2A Protocol Inspector
quote: The Inspector is a web-based tool that fetches Agent Cards, performs basic spec-compliance checks, provides live chat, and shows raw JSON-RPC traffic in a debug console.

title: [Feat]: Add client-side protocol versioning in payloads
quote: Issue #1189 says the upcoming 1.0 release introduces breaking changes and proposes protocol_version fields so servers can translate between 0.3 and 1.0 clients for backwards compatibility.

title: 1.0 Release
quote: The Python SDK's 1.0 release umbrella issue says 1.0 spec support and SDK API breaking changes are being developed in the 1.0-dev branch.

title: [Bug]: DefaultRequestHandler hangs on_message_send for consumer failures
quote: Issue #609 reports that DefaultRequestHandler can hang on_message_send when consumer-side failures occur, showing that edge-case robustness was still being fixed in the Python SDK.

title: [Feat]: Implement A2A Authentication Handling in A2A Inspector
quote: Issue #127 says the Inspector validates Agent Card format and returned JSON-RPC but does not yet use Agent Card security schemes to perform A2A authentication flows.

title: [Bug]: Inconsistency for task.id vs contextId or messageId
quote: Issue #1335 argues that task object naming is inconsistent because Task uses id while Message, Context, and Artifact use messageId, contextId, and artifactId.

title: What is the Agent2Agent (A2A) Protocol?
quote: Builder wrote in May 2025 that the spec was barely out of the oven, while still calling it a concrete step toward less brittle, more composable agent workflows.

title: Vendor-Neutral AI Agent Interoperability Project’s Participants Include Microsoft, Google, AWS, Salesforce
quote: TechRepublic described the Linux Foundation launch as a nonprofit hub for the A2A protocol after Google announced it in April 2025.
